The Waking Sea

After the assaults on both Lukesh and now Sendis, I fear for my other companion of old, the dwarf Tovez. He may be in grave danger. {%HeroName%}, you must go to Orzammar and bring him safely back to Kaiten.
Find Ravi's old companion, the dwarf Tovez
Shipwrecked!
Swim to the safety of the Waking Sea coast
Surrounded!
Fight off the attackers and continue heading inland
Shipwrecked!
Despite your heroic efforts, {%HeroName%}, that powerful storm smashed our ship on the rocky shoals of this island. We have to get to shore and find our bearings.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
What is this?
Ah, poor Yendrick, a good sailor. Not good enough to prevent this disaster, but good enough to have some supplies on him.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
Another shipwreck!
We aren't the first to wreck on these shoals. No survivors here.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
Looking Around
Good thinking. Keep looking for supplies; we'll need them.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
More Supplies!
What a find! These items will surely help, for who knows what inhabitants we might encounter.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
A Dreadful Omen
I recognize this sailor. Poor Lucretion. These spear and axe wounds look pretty fresh, we'd best be careful.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
Surrounded!
An ambush! They'll never take us alive!
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
Sacrifice
Whose blood is on this altar? No friend of ours, I hope.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
Quest Complete
We need to get inland and find a more defensible position.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
Heading Inland
We need to regroup and recover. Perhaps one of those two ruins up ahead would make decent shelter.
Start heading inland toward the port town of Jainen
Escape to the Ruins
Travel toward the ruins to escape your pursuers
A Thick Thicket
Cross to the other side of the thicket and continue toward the coast
Left or right? Which ruin should we head toward? Quickly, the natives are following us!
It certainly seems this way will offer more shelter.
Higher ground. Good idea; I'll be able to get my bearings.
Who knows what foes may be hiding in this thicket. Shall we go around or through it?
I like your style. I'll... erm... watch your back.
Marauders! Good thing we didn't charge right into their camp!
Good eyes; you found a supply cache.
Just a few more steps and we can get our bearings from higher ground.
I see a road to the north... but our pursuers are upon us! Prepare yourself.
A dead end! We'll have to fight our way back onto the path.
Just a few more steps and we'll be there.
They've caught up to us, but at least we can pick them off a few at a time.
Quest Complete
Excellent, I can see for miles from here. Just a moment, let me get my bearings and I can get us out of this mess.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
Toward Jainen
Ah, I know where we are now. We should head north to the city of Jainen. It is a port town, and we can get a boat there to Orzammar.
Continue your journey north toward Jainen
The Estate
Search the estate for clues on the whereabouts of its inhabitants
Dragon Cultists
Defeat the dragon cultists that ambushed your party and go towards the nests
Dragonling Nests
Clear a path through the dragonling nests to reach the coast
Our pursuers seem to have given up on us. I wonder what this desolate place holds that they fear.
This molten rock breaks right up through the earth. Like dragon's teeth!
These notes mention First Enchanter Jendrik, the head of the Circle in Jainen. I wonder why?
What is this? These notes detail First Enchanter Jendrik's habits and friends.
More notes about First Enchanter Jendrik... They were keeping a close eye on him.
Nobody has been to this estate recently. It wasn't even looted. Look at what the owners left behind!
The owners of this estate are likely long dead... or they became cultists.
Either way, they won't be needing this anymore.
Dragon cultists! I should have known!
We'll have to fight our way through the dragonling nests to get to the coast. Be ready!
I'm amazed we made it through there alive, {%HeroName%}. Your skills in battle are amazing. You remind me of someone: me!
Quest Complete

The outlying estates have been taken over by dragon cultists. This does not bode well for Jainen. We must get there quickly. If we can reach the coast, we should be able to find Ceirad's Rock. That will lead us to Jainen.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
North Jainen Coast
We need to follow the coastline to the west toward Ceirad's Rock. From this landmark, we should be able to see the port city of Jainen.
Travel to Ceirad's Rock to see how far it is to Jainen
Drake Nests
Either cut through or go around the drake nests
Bandits
Defeat the bandits blocking the path
Cierad's Rock
Reach Cierad's Rock
Another drake nest! Let's wipe these out before they get any bigger.
This island is infested with cultists! Here's hoping Jainen is still holding fast against them.
What has the animals here all riled up? Watch it: There may be more cultists nearby.
Looks like bandits up ahead. Steady yourself!
I think that is Cierad's Rock over there!
Quest Complete
There is the rock! We are close to Jainen... and hopefully a boat to Orzammar.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
Reach Jainen
I can see the buildings of Jainen up ahead. Soon we'll be off this blighted rock and on our way to find Tovez.
Search the Jainen port to see if you can find a boat
The Circle
Talk to the Circle Mages to learn more about Jainen
Make Way
Cross the drake nests to reach the southern port
Enchanted
Find First Enchanter Jendrik
This isn't right. Jainen is a busy port city... but there's no one here.
We need to check that port and see if we can find a boat.
There is the Circle. We should go see if First Enchanter Jendrik is alright.
It's quiet... too quiet.
Those mages were not themselves! They were dominated by demons.
I knew it: demons everywhere! We need to find Jendrik, if we are not too late already.
The First Enchanter Jendrik thanks you. I don't know how much longer he could have resisted these demons.
Jendrik says the leader of the dragon cultists here, Deymour... he's gone mad with power!
A note from someone called Deymour! It says to destroy the boats at the northern port. We got here too late.
We will have to head south. Hopefully we can reach a boat before this madman destroys them all.
No boats here. We will have to check the south port. Hopefully we'll find something there.
We should check on Jendrik before heading through Jainen.
Watch out! There are drake nests are up ahead. I just hope Deymour isn't there.
More drake nests! We have to fight through to get to the southern port.
Another note from Deymour. It is addressed to someone named Guillen. Apparently Guillen was to execute First Enchanter Jendrik today.
Good thing we got there when we did; it was supposed to happen a few minutes ago.
According to the note, it already happened a few minutes ago.
Quest Complete
First Enchanter Jendrik is probably dead. We need to get to Tovez before he meets the same fate.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
Quest Complete
Jendrik is wounded, so he won't be of much help physically. But he's safer with us than the demons.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
South Jainen Coast
Deymour's forces here are too strong for us to escape safely. We need to cripple them before we attempt to escape this isle.
Defeat the forces surrounding the ports and discover where Deymour is headed
Find Deymour
Look for clues about Deymour's plans
Guillen
Defeat Guillen and continue to pursue Deymour
Escape Route
Look for boats on the western shore.
There are more ports to the west here. We should check to see if there are any boats we can use.
No boats here. Undoubtedly Deymour's work.
A note! Looks like Guillen was supposed stop us and then meet with Deymour... and you'll never guess where.
In Orzammar!
Quest Complete
We need to catch Guillen's boat at the south port. Let's finally get off this rock.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
Jainen Port
Let's see if we can find a ship that doesn't leak like a sieve. Something with decent armament wouldn't be bad, either.
Find a ship to set sail for Orzammar
Pass Through
Move through the town on the way to the nests
Drake Nests
Wipe out the drake nests to clear a path to the docks
Deymour
Chase down and defeat Deymour
There are more drake nests here; we need to wipe them out. I doubt we'd make it all the way to Orzammar on scorched sails.
Deymour is up ahead, preparing to leave for Orzammar. If we hurry, we might still catch him.
He didn't see that coming.
Quest Complete

I'll take Deymour back to Ravi. I expect the Viscount will want to... talk with him. Take Lukesh and meet with Tovez at Orzammar. Here, take this signet ring; it will allow you entrance into the underground city. Good luck, my friend.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
Ferelden Coast
Get to the Imperial Highway. It is the fastest way to get to Orzammar. Watch out for bandits.
Travel on the Imperial Highway toward Orzammar.
Bandits
Make a Decision regarding the Bandits
Cease-fire?
Get through the darkspawn invasion to continue your journey toward Orzammar
Clean Up
Clear out the darkspawn in the bandit camp
This doesn't look right.
The bandits are calling a cease-fire! Darkspawn are attacking their camp!
Should we go west to their camp and aid them or just let the darkspawn take care of them?
You are right. We cannot turn a blind eye wherever the darkspawn attack.
That's a bit callous, but understandable. We don't have time for this.
That's it? Just one group? No problem.
An ambush! And they have ogres! This is not good.
Ogres! Be careful!
That was incredible. Let's go find Tovez.
We were too late. Everyone is dead.
The camp is clear and the bandits are saved. You are made of tough stuff, {%HeroName%}.
More casualties of the darkspawn. If only we'd come sooner...
Quest Complete
The Imperial Highway! It should be easy going all the way to the gates of Orzammar.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
Either help or ignore the bandits on the road to Orzammar
Gherlen's Pass
This is treacherous terrain. I do not like the look of these cliffs. I cannot see what they hide.
Continue your journey through Gherlen's Pass to reach the Gates of Orzammar.
Ogres!
Defeat the ogres blocking your way through Gherlen's Pass
It's quiet. Too quiet.
Ogres, up on the cliffs!
Keep your eyes open; there are probably more.
Here they come! What are all these ogres doing aboveground?
That was brutal. Let's get out of here.
Never have I seen this pass so overrun with darkspawn.
Even when Ravi's uncle turned to an abomination, it was never this bad. We need to get to the bottom of this.
This reminds me of that massive ogre I fought in that cave in the free marches.
What's that over there?
I think I saw a glint in this crevice over there.
Do you see it?
Quest Complete
We are almost there. The gates should be just around this bend.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
Gates of Orzammar
We are very close to the gates. Shouldn't be too long now.
Enter Orzammar and continue your search for Tovez.
Camp Killer
Destroy the darkpawn encampment blocking the path to Orzammar
A Demonstration
Kill the darkspawn leader to convince the guards to open the gates
I see the gates of Orzammar up ahead, but there is a massive darkspawn encampment guarding it!
We should free the captives here as soon as possible.
We're here to help. Stay close!
We were too late. Prepare to defend yourself!
We saved a few dwarves, but several were killed in the battle.
We need to take down the leader before the dwarven guards will open the gates.
Sure, now they open the gates - after you've taken care of the problem for them.
Quest Complete
You finally made it. Good luck finding Tovez, though. He'll probably find you first.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
Orzammar
With a darkspawn encampment outside the front door, Tovez must be in danger! We need to find him as soon as possible!
Continue the search for Tovez.
Search Tovez's House
Look for clues as to Tovez's whereabouts
The Proving Grounds
Defeat the Carta rogues in the Proving Grounds
The Deep Roads
Find the Entrance to the Deep Roads
Hm. This is Tovez's place. No one is here.
Tovez? I haven't seen him in days.
Tovez? That cloudgazer? Nay, haven't seen him.
This statue is of Beirus, a true living paragon of Orzammar. Apparently his prowess at the forge rivals that of Caridin.
Get out, topsider. You don't belong here.
Tovez? Last I saw he was hanging around in Dust Town.
The carta are everywhere these days. If only Beirus were here...
When Beirus left, he said he would fix Caridin's greatest failure. I didn't know Caridin had failed.
Hey, topsider. Over here.
My sons went down into the Deep Roads looking for the tossers that defaced our ancestor's statue. Can you find them?
Welcome to Dust Town, topsider. Pardon us as we help ourselves to your coin.
Perhaps we should help them see the error of their ways.
Think you're tough stuff? Meet us at the Proving. We'll see who the real champion is.
Welcome to the Proving. Here we settle disagreements in the most civilized manner possible. In violent combat.
You're breathing smoke if you think you can stand against us.
You did pretty well against those dusters. I hear you're looking for me, {%HeroName%}... Lukesh? What are you doing here?
Indeed it is I. You are looking particularly grimy. Are there no baths in Orzammar?
Nice to see you too, you crusty old bastard. What brings you here?
...I see.
There are demonic disturbances on the surface. Ravi was worried you were in trouble.
We were even more apprehensive when we saw the darkspawn camped outside.
There were darkspawn camped outside? ... I have other concerns.
I'm not gallavanting off to Kaiten until I've gotten to the bottom of Beirus' disappearance into the Deep Roads.
I can't let you go into the deep roads without a dwarven escort. Wait, Tovez?
If Tovez vouches for you, fine. Careful, the deep roads are dangerous.
Quest Complete

Interesting. It seems these Carta dwarves are not just in it for the usual money and power. There is something deeper here. I feel it is related to the other problems we've seen thus far, and so our paths must diverge here. {%HeroName%}, you help Tovez in the Deep Roads, and I will investigate the Carta matter here.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
The Deep Roads
Let's see if we can find any clues about Beirus' whereabouts outside of Orzammar. We need to be careful though, as the Deep Roads are never safe.
Make your way through the deep roads to find Beirus.
Spiders!
Get past the spiders in the Deep Roads
Make it Through
Safely find your way through the Deep Roads and find information on Beirus
The Deep Roads
I've been coming down into the Deep Roads looking for Beirus. During my last expedition, I found clues that led me here. You're lucky I was in Orzammar to resupply. I had completely run out of ale.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
...I must say I am surprised to hear that Sendis would ask for Ravi's help.
Iselle died in childbirth. Sendis has always blamed Ravi for his sister's death.
It looks like the road ahead is broken, but there's a cave entrance to the side over here.
I can see something up ahead. I wonder if that can give us any idea where Beirus went.
These deep stalkers are freshly killed. Something isn't right here.
Look out, it's an ambush!
Let's see if this path can take us to any clues.
The Deep Roads are riddled with caves. Some are shortcuts... usually to an early demise.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
This narrow passage seems to go somewhere. It looks dangerous, but we have no other choice.
The Deep Roads
Watch out: thaig spiders. We could go straight through the nest or keep to the edges. The edges should be safer.
Blast it! Look what you've gotten us into. Brace yourself!
Thank the Stones we're finally out of that nest.
Let's see if we can get back on the roads. These caves are filled with trouble.
We need to get off the road again. It's a dead end up ahead.
Let's try this side passage.
The Deep Roads used to be so much easier to navigate. Back when they were... you know... roads.
It looks like someone has been here recently, and there are tracks leading to the ruins over there. Let's check it out.
Some old food scraps; Beirus was here recently. We're getting close.
Quickly, to the bridge over there. We'll have to plow through those darkspawn.
The Deep Roads
I think we're nearing Davroken Thaig. Of course! This must be where Beirus was heading. Let's see what lies ahead.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
Helping a Brother
Help Falral find his brother.
Falral
Assist Falral in his search for his brother
Oh wow, I thought no one would ever come along. I am Falral.
I went down here looking for the tossers that defaced the statue of our paragon ancestor Tragot.
I was with my brother, Arrar, when we were ambushed by a group of darkspawn and separated.
Can you help me find him? Unfortunately, I have nothing to offer you.
I don't see any sign of him. Can we keep looking?
Just a pile of bones. This can't be Arrar.
I never should have taken Arrar down here with me. Where could he be?
The Deep Roads
By the Stones, here lies Arrar, slain by who knows what. Please take his ring with my thanks.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
An Interesting Item
What is this? There seems to be a badge of some sort on the ground. From the looks of it, it is an old dwarven paragon's badge. I wonder who it belongs to.
Learn more about the badge found on the floor of the Deep Roads.
Give up the badge to the bandits
Sell It?
Choosing this will give the badge to the bandits. Do you want to continue?
Keep the paragon badge
Keep It?
Choosing this will keep the badge and not sell to the bandits. Do you want to continue?
A Proposition
Decide what to do with the badge
Ah, I see you've found something valuable. I think that belongs to us, but I'll be kind today.
I'll make you a deal. I have 1000 gold with your name on it if you give that to me. Otherwise we'll have to take it from you by other means.
You'll pay for this. Attack!
Sodding looters. Let's see if anybody in Orzammar knows what this badge is.
This string should no longer display.
It was nice doing business with you.
The Deep Roads
Nice doing business with you.
What is this?
Wait, this paragon seal belonged to my ancestor, Tragot. How did you get this?
We found this badge out in the Deep Roads.
I will gladly pay you for this. Many thanks to you, {%HeroName%}.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
Davroken Thaig
The tracks lead out of the Deep Roads into Davroken Thaig. Let's get moving.
Search the Davroken Thaig for signs of Beirus.
Golems!
Fight through the golem attacks
Beirus
Find Beirus in the Davroken Thaig
Chase!
Pursue your quarry
Davroken Thaig
Davroken Thaig was lost to the darkspawn centuries ago. I wonder what it is that brought Beirus here to this Stone-forsaken place.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
Something is wrong with these golems. They're twisted, misshapen.
Lava everywhere. Much of Davroken Thaig has been lost to the Stone... really, really hot Stone.
See that bridge? That should get us over the lava flows.
I can see the lights of a camp up ahead. It has to be Beirus.
The ancient golems were not so vicious and brutal as these.
I hope that I'm wrong, but I think Beirus' tampering unleashed these monstrosities.
Golems can only be created by sacrificing other dwarves. How is he creating all these abominations?
We are not too far now. That definitely looks like a campsite.
Hello, Tovez. Do you like my golems?
Beirus? You created these monsters?
Caridin was a fool. I have improved his methods. Join me and you will understand.
You are not the paragon I once looked up to. This ends now.
I am sorry, Tovez. You will join us or be crushed under our feet.
You don't understand. I have found a better way. You cannot stop what I have begun.
He's getting away! We cannot let this continue.
Beirus is almost unrecognizable. What did he do to himself?
What does he mean by "we don't understand"?
How has he been creating these golems?
Where has he been getting dwarves for subjects?
This bridge is highly unstable. We'll have to go around.
Quest Complete
He can only have gone through here. Let's make sure that none of those infernal things leave this thaig.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
An Unlikely Encounter
I hear shouting up ahead. Be ready for anything.
Help find Isalda in the Davroken Thaig. Later on, check up with Tharin Highmount in Kirkwall.
Tharin
Find Tharin Highmount
Isalda
Find out what happened to Isalda
A Conclusion
Meet up with Tharin Highmount later on.
Look, over there! A dwarf is pinned in by those golems.
Tharin? What in the Stones are you doing here?
Searching. Isalda is here somewhere, I know it. Will you help me?
Isalda was a servant of my house... or was until my father cast her into the Deep Roads.
If only we had been discreet. I should have listened; we could have fled to the surface.
Isalda... no...
She still breathes. Perhaps she will yet live.
There is nothing we can do for her.
Quest Complete
You found Tharin Highmount's love Isalda, badly wounded by these misshapen golems. Tharin is taking her to the surface where they can live together away from his family.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
Quest Complete
You found Tharin Highmount's love Isalda, crushed to death by darkspawn golems. Beirus has much to answer for with his dabbling.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
Quest Complete
It is good to see Tharin and Isalda doing well. Here's hoping we run into them again
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
Quest Complete
The stones can be cruel sometimes. Let's go, we must continue our journey.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
{%HeroName%}, Tovez? Good to see you.
Tharin Highmount, I am glad you and Isalda made it out of the Deep Roads safely.
Thanks for helping us. We miss Orzammar, but at least we're together now.
It was good to see you two, try and stay out of trouble.
{%HeroName%}... Tovez? What're you doing here?
Tharin Highmount, you seem... out of sorts.
The surface air does not agree with me. Better than dead in the deep roads, I suppose.
That is true... Pass me some of that ale, friend.
Beirus' Retreat
Beirus has fled, and we need to get answers from him. He can't have gone far.
Make your way into Beirus' retreat.
The End of the Road
Defeat Beirus and his golems
Beirus' Retreat
This whole thaig is swarming with golems. How long has Beirus been creating these? How did he create them all himself? And where did all this blood come from?
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
Time has not been kind to Davroken Thaig.
Darkspawn and golems working together... What is going on?
Do you see my genius now? No longer will dwarves be used as husks for golems.
No... It can't be...
The darkspawn are a nearly unlimited resource.
What better use for them than as weapons against their own kind?
Allow me to demonstrate.
This is madness. Where is Beirus now? We have to find him and stop this.
This is the only way he could have gone...
That looks like a forge over there.
That must be where he's smelting lyrium to make the golems.
If you get in my way, I will have to destroy you.
You're making golems from darkspawn?
This is the only way we can defeat the darkspawn scourge.
You are insane! You cannot trust these darkspawn. They'll turn on you.
Dark patron... why have you forsaken... me...
What is he talking about? Let's get this pile of stone back to Kaiten.
Quest Complete
Now that we have Beirus in custody, we need to get back to Ravi and report what has transpired here. It has to do with the rest of this madness somehow.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
Kirkwall Docks

{%HeroName%}! Tovez! I am glad to see you. Sendis and I have been trying to find you in the deep roads, a task easier said than done. Ahh, I see you have Beirus with you; I will take him with me back to Kaiten for safe keeping. Sendis will accompany you two to Kirkwall. Lukesh sent a message several days ago, saying he needed assistance there.
Search the Kirkwall docks for Lukesh
Choose Your Informant
Find someone who has seen Lukesh
Find More Information
Earn the favor of your informant to learn more about Lukesh’s whereabouts
Meet at Lowtown
Travel to Lowtown to receive information about Lukesh
Ask the Kirkwall guards for help
Side with the Guard
Choosing this will remove Marsa from your party. Do you want to continue?
Side with the Coterie by attacking the guards
Side with the Coterie
Choosing this will remove Varence from your party. Do you want to continue?
Decide to find Lukesh without any help
Go It Alone
Choosing this will remove Korznik your party. Do you want to continue?
Kirkwall Docks
Let's get looking. The last time I was here I was rescuing Iselle from thugs in Lowtown. Can't say I've missed the place.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
We should ask around about Lukesh.
Let's ask that guard. It doesn't look like he's doing much of anything.
...and he just walked away. Fantastic.
Perhaps he has other business to attend to?
Wasn't there a guard over there? He did nothing about that ambush!
We're looking for Lukesh, a friend of ours. Old guy, grey beard, robes...
What do I look like? A tour guide? I don't have time for this.
Maybe we'll have better luck asking around at the guard barracks?
You are looking for Lukesh? The guards are useless. We could speak more freely at my tent.
I only trust these long-ears about as far as I can kick them.
Excuse me?
Present company excluded.
The Kirkwall guard is vile and corrupt. There is no way I am working with them again.
Seriously? Despite my warning, you're working with the guard? I am out of here.
The Coterie are criminals, working with them is not a good idea...
So I may have some news about Lukesh... but I don't do favors.
There are some guards that... aren't in our employ, but patrol the docks. Take them out and we can talk again.
Perhaps we could force the Coterie to tell us where Lukesh is instead of fighting those who would protect the city?
Fight the Coterie? You're insane.
We either do the Coterie's dirty work, or we wage war against the Coterie. Your call, {%HeroName%}.
I'll not have these deaths on my conscience. If you insist on killing innocent guards, I will take my leave.
This isn't right. Do what you feel you must, but I will not be part of it.
Fighting the Coterie isn't my idea of courage. More like... suicide.
I've got better things to do. Have fun storming their lair.
Let's get going. Lowtown is this way.
Another ambush, of course.
Turn around now, outsiders. You're not welcome in Lowtown.
Can we ever go anywhere without having to fight a whole army?
Quest complete
I can't imagine Lowtown getting any less dangerous, so be on the lookout for trouble.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
Kirkwall Docks
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
Get information about the whereabouts of Lukesh.
Clean Up
Clear out the bandits as a favor to the guards
Deal With It
Pacify the rogue guards near the temple
A Rescue
Defeat the Coterie and find Geiral
My name is Atria, how can I help you... Lukesh? That name sounds familiar.
I'd have my sources look into it, but we have a crisis on our hands.
Help us out, and we'll help you. There are several bandits running amok in the market.
The market is just north of here. Let's get moving.
Let's check back and see if they have any news.
Another ruckus near the temple! Guards have gone rogue and joined the raiders!
I got here as soon as I could. One of my contacts, Geiral, was asking around about Lukesh.
The Coterie have gotten to Geiral first. They want that information, but I don't know what for.
They are in the market near the docks. We must act quickly.
The market is filled with Coterie. We must save Geiral.
Our agent is nowhere to be found. This is worse than I thought.
We recently learned the Coterie are meeting with a powerful merchant at the Kaperk mansion.
They must have Geiral with them. The mansion is in the city square to the northeast.
Good to see you in one piece, Geiral. Thank you {%HeroName%}.
I need to follow up on a few things, but meet me near lowtown soon.
Thank you again, {%HeroName%}. Geiral learned that Lukesh was investigating in Kirkwall.
I know this is not news to you.
However, we also discovered he was last seen at an old abandoned building in Lowtown.
Follow the marks on this map, you should be able to find it. Good luck.
Kirkwall Docks
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
Get information about the whereabouts of Lukesh.
Clean Up
Clear out the bandits as a favor to the Coterie
Take the Cache
Steal the cache of weapons for the Coterie
The Final Blow
Help out the Coterie at the guard headquarters
I sure hope this is worth it. Let's go find that elf.
We did your dirty work, now out with it.
Are you joking? I have another task for you first.
A few ex-associates are elbowing in with a rival venture. I'm sure you can fix it.
Huh. Looks like a note from knifeears.
Come meet me in the courtyard.
There he is.
There are a large number of guards moving a cache of weapons on the west side of this courtyard.
Take out the guards and get us those weapons. Once you do that, I have some plans for you.
The cache is over there. Let's get it.
Now that we have the supplies, we can take down the rest of the loyal guards in the headquarters.
If we strike while the guards are in disarray, they won't know what hit them.
I'll get the information you need and meet you by Lowtown.
Now tell us where Lukesh is!
Your friend appears to have gotten himself in trouble with the carta down in Lowtown.
Carta, in Kirkwall? Or more shady characters like yourself?
We had nothing to do with it, honestly. He was ambushed at a carta hideout.
I'll mark it on your map. You do good work, {%HeroName%}. If you ever need more, let me know.
Kirkwall Docks
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
Get information about the whereabouts of Lukesh.
Where is he?
Find Jebbry for information about Lukesh
Mistaken Identity
Find some information from a contact at the temple
The Rescue
Rescue Goran from the Coterie
Go a little easier on these knifeears, {%HeroName%}, it is hard to interrogate the dead.
I saw a few of his friends run off to the northwest. They might know something.
They're just ahead, beyond all these other ruffians.
Keep going, we don't want to lose these guys.
Now we've got them cornered! Surrender!
You're in over your head, stubby.
Where's Lukesh? Grey beard, green robes...
I don't know what you're talking about!
Maybe my AXE will help bring back some memories...
Okay, okay, talk to the Jebbry in the nearby market, he's the brains. Don't kill me...
Ah. Jebbry, I presume? Where is Lukesh?
The Coterie have nothing to do with Lukesh. He avoided us altogether.
Go talk to Goran at the temple, I know he and Lukesh had a chat. Watch out for gangs.
The temple is up this way.
Hello, Marsa. I told you never to come back to Kirkwall.
Kedra? I'm going to enjoy smashing your traitorous face in.
That must be Gorash over there.
What do you want from me, son of the stone?
Lukesh spoke to you, old man. We need to find him.
He did not speak to me, but Gorash, my brother. I am Yammis.
If you wish to speak to him, you'll have to rescue him from the clutches of the Coterie.
They are keeping Gorash in the tower over here.
Yammis, it is good to see you. Who is this with you?
These good folk are looking for Lukesh. Do you know where he went?
Indeed, he went down to Lowtown. I can mark the building on your map. There.
Thank you for your help.
Lowtown is up ahead. I hope Lukesh is still there.
Marsa Leaves
Varence Leaves
Korznik Leaves
Kirkwall Lowtown
Let's Go find Hagen.
Explore Lowtown for signs of Lukesh or Hagen.
Visit Hagen’s Hideout
Explore Hagen’s Hideout for clues about Hagen’s whereabouts
Friend or Foe
Defeat and deal with Hagen Mortren
Leaving So Soon?
Make your way out of Kirkwall
Decide to kill Hagen
The Final Blow
Choosing this will kill Hagen and take his things. Do you want to continue?
Decide to save Hagen
An Uneasy Alliance
Choosing this will add Hagen Mortren to your party. Do you want to continue?
Kirkwall Lowtown
We're surely in Lowtown now, judging from the smell. Let's get to the building and then find Lukesh.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
It looks like lots of people just passed through here. Be on the lookout for trouble.
The bandits are coming out. I'm ready to crack a few heads in.
The building is to the north around this corner. I hope Lukesh is still there.
This way.
Let's stay off of the roads. We'll be less likely to run into large groups of bandits.
This is the building marked on the map. It's looks abandoned, but we should check it out for clues.
There's nothing in here. What's that?
We've got trouble. Bandits.
Your group is too curious. Leave Lowtown or face the consequences.
We greatly outnumber you. Leave while you still can.
He's a slippery little shaft rat. Let's get him.
We warned you to turn around. You won't make it out of Lowtown alive.
Slipped through our fingers again! We need to back him into a corner.
What are these dark elves? They aren't city or Dalish elves.
Nice try, but you can't win.
Flank him, we've almost got him now!
Backed into a corner. You have nowhere to go.
Let's gut him.
No, wait. I can help you...
Help us? You were trying to kill us a moment ago.
Prove your worth. Where is Lukesh? Spit it out!
It's those tainted elves. They hired us to stop you from finding Lukesh.
Tainted? They're darkspawn? You were helping darkspawn?
I'd work for you instead in a heartbeat!
And betray us in the next. Your call, {%HeroName%}. Kill him or keep him.
I swear on my dead relatives - and even on the ones who are not feeling too good - I am your man forever, {%HeroName%}!
The tainted elves have Lukesh in the forest. Let's go get him.
Ouch. He didn't stand a chance.
If the tainted elves in the forest have Lukesh, we have to go find him.
Let's get out of the city and find Lukesh before those tainted elves do something terrible to him.
Quest complete
If Lukesh is in that forest, we better be going.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
Hagen's Belt
It seems as if Hagan can aid us, even in death. His belt looks very interesting. Let's take it.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
Hagen is Alive
Hagen is Dead
Pick up the Trail
We need to head into the Vimmark Wasteland and find Lukesh!
Make your way through Kirkwall to find Lukesh.
Reunion
Find your missing companion
Village
Get to the village outskirts
There is something very wrong about these tainted elves, {%HeroName%}.
What was it that gave it away, the blood magic, or the part where they were trying to kill us?
Blood magic isn't uncommon among Dalish elves, but these seemed to have a form of the taint in them.
What? You saying their coin isn't good?
No, they seemed to have some form of the darkspawn taint in them.
Wait, over there by the river. Is that Marsa?
I'd recognize the gleam of that blade anywhere. Looks like she's got trouble.
We've got you cornered now, Marsa. You are dead.
{%HeroName%}?
You have bad timing.
None of these guards deserve to live.
I'm not sure.
Darkspawn! In the trees!
Leave these traitors to their fate. Saves us the trouble.
Rescue the Kirkwall guards from the darkspawn?
Fight the Darkspawn
Choosing this will anger Marsa. Do you want to continue?
You can't be serious.
You take the left flank, we'll take the riverside.
Whatever. At least we don't have to fight alongside them.
I can't believe we just helped those guards... where did they go?
Looks like they didn't want to have to thank you or {%HeroName%}.
Leave the Kirkwall guards to the darkspawn?
Let the Guards Die
Choosing this will please Marsa. Do you want to continue?
Come back, help! Auuugh!
We're not out of this yet, there are more darkspawn ahead.
Good riddance.
I hope you found what you were looking for, {%HeroName%}.
Varence! What have you been doing out here?
There is a small village on the outskirts being oppressed by greedy raiders. I tried to help them, but I admit I am not strong enough. I could use your help.
That's a lot of darkspawn down there. Perhaps we should go around.
Is that... Korznik? Surrounded by darkspawn? This should be good.
It is Korznik! We should give him a hand with those hurlocks.
{%HeroName%}? Welcome to the party.
I see you found some kindred spirits.
There's a few more hurlocks behind you. I'm sure they'd love to chat.
So, how did negotiations with the Coterie go?
Smashingly.
We got some tips on where Lukesh might be. Let's get going.
Quest complete
You've reached the village.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
Vimmark Village
There is a small village on the outskirts being oppressed by greedy raiders.
Investigate the village
Investigate the Village
Decide what to do about the raiders
Darkspawn Scourge
Wipe out the darkspawn in the forest
Kill and Tell
Return to the village
I've already given you most of my food! My family needs the rest to survive!
We could just leave your pitiful village to the darkspawn.
Who is this? Tough guys, eh? Move along and there'll be no trouble.
Leave the village in the hands of raiders?
Leave the village
Choosing this will leave the villagers in the hands of raiders. Do you want to continue?
That's right. It is none of your business.
Those villagers will starve. How could you be so heartless, {%HeroName%}?
Liberate the village of raiders?
Liberate the village
Choose this to fight off the raiders holding the village hostage. Do you want to continue?
You'll regret this moment.
You idiots!
You're welcome... wait, what?
They were the only thing holding back the darkspawn. We'll all be dead in a week.
Blast it! Nothing for it... where are the darkspawn? We'll clear them out.
Apparently the darkspawn are coming from that forest to the east.
Are we, uh, really going in there?
Wait, shouldn't we be looking for Lukesh?
It's the least we can do. That village will be wiped out if we don't.
We should let the villagers know that we've wiped out this group of darkspawn.
We should also see if they've seen Lukesh.
Back again? Clear off, you are not needed here.
We've cleared the forest of darkspawn. It is you thugs who are no longer needed.
We aren't going anywhere. Kill them all.
Quest complete
The villagers are safe from the darkspawn plague. They said that Lukesh had headed to the nearby village some time ago.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
Abandoned Settlement
Look for signs of Lukesh in the nearby village.
Look for signs of Lukesh in the nearby village.
Search the Settlement
Find some sign of Lukesh's passage in the village
It appears that those raiders were not making idle threats. This village is devoid of life.
Perhaps. But this is where the villagers said Lukesh went. Let's look around.
It's blasted hard to spot darkspawn here, what with all these dead bodies.
Wait, there is a carving in the stone here.

I am going to Sundermount to meet with the strange shadow elves. There is some great evil there, and I fear I shall not return. I leave this message in hopes that {%HeroName%} or another friend may find it. -Lukesh
Quest complete
You have found a message from Lukesh. You must meet with him at Sundermount.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
Sundermount
Lukesh's note indicated he was going to Sundermount but was concerned for his safety. It is imperative we get there as soon as possible, we must learn what has become of him.
Get to Sundermount to meet Lukesh.
Get to Sundermount
Find where Lukesh has gone.
Sundermount is a dangerous place, filled with ancient horrors unleashed for a battle waged long ago.
What possessed Lukesh to come here? We'd best be cautious.
Cautious? I can't see anything in this blasted mist!
Do you see those shadowy figures up ahead?
Looks like we'll have go right through them.
Good idea. Axe end first!
These elves seem to be protecting something
Yeah, where they stashed Lukesh. I think I see a mine or something ahead.
Elves? Mining? How... curious.
You should leave whence you came. You do not want to enter here.
I'm looking for a buddy of mine. Old and grizzled guy in green robes?
Unfortunate. You will suffer his fate, then.
I am so tired of asking this. Where. Is. Lukesh?
They took him into the mines. The others will kill you before you find him, though.
We should gut him, otherwise he might get more of his buddies.
Bring them on, I could use a challenge.
Leave no witnesses?
Leave no witnesses?
Kill the elf captive?
Have mercy?
Have mercy?
Leave the elf alive?
Ah, this is why they hired the carta. These are all dwarven mining implements.
But... they don't appear to be mining for ore or minerals. What are they doing?
Lukesh?! Thank the maker, you're alive.
We need to get out of here. Now.
Wait, what?
I will explain on the way. This way, there is no time to lose.
Quest complete
You have rescued Lukesh, but now you must follow him to the surface.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
Ravi? How did you find us?
I've been following your trail of destruction from Kirkwall. It wasn't hard.
I believe we are very close to finding Lukesh.
Oh? Well then, {%HeroName%}, lead the way.
Darkspawn in Sundermount. This brings me back.
Ah, there is no better reunion than in battle.
Save your reminiscing and lollygagging for later.
Run!
Lukesh was captured by the shadowy elves, but there is something more sinister lurking in the background. Time to get to the surface before it catches up with you.
Escape the Mines.
Escape the Mines.
Fight your way out of the mines to the surface.
These elves... they are worshipping some sinister power. They are going to unleash it soon.
It is too late, I hear it now!
grrrRRRAAAARRRR!!!!
What... is that???
Quickly, before it returns. We must get out of here!
Those elves... they just let it devour them... some kind of terrible blood magic.
I think it was stronger that time, the elf sacrifices are giving it power!
We must pursue and strike before it is too strong for us!
What a horrible pile of rot, bones, and stink.
Yes... we must take it back with us.
Take it with us???
Very well, if you think it will help. But.... ugh.
Return to {%castle_name%}
Take the grotesque trophy back to your estate.
{%castle_name%} is under siege! What are Qunari doing here?
We need to get through them, I need a chance to study these remains in safety.
You have defeated my servants, but I will be more than a match for you.
I have learned enough here. Do what you will.
He's gone!
A darkspawn that can speak, that is strange.
Ravi! {%HeroName%}! Come quickly!
Learn from Lukesh
Find out what Lukesh has discovered.
The dragon was animated by the pride demon, the one that possessed Khedra.
What? No, we defeated that demon years ago, it can't be.
The demon's soul was shattered, but the pieces embedded themselves in other beings.
One in the bone dragon, Tianne, Deymour, and Soleil... there is yet one more.
Quest complete
You and your companions are back at {%castle_name%}, but there is still much to be done. The Pride Demon that was defeated by Ravi and his friends has returned to the world. It must be destroyed.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
Oh hello. Run from one problem only to be faced by four more.
Ah, I can smell trees. We must be close to an exit.
How can you smell anything over this rotten pile of... lookout, spiders!
Is there a cave in Thedas that is NOT infested with these blasted spiders?
Ahh, treasure. Perhaps all those venom-filled bites were worth it, then.
I have never been so glad to leave a cave in my life.
Lukesh has found a way to hurt the Pride Demon. You must go to him in Kaiten. Hurry, as we do not have much time.
Go to Kaiten to see Lukesh
Meet Up
Go see Lukesh in Kaiten
Evra's Armor

I have found an answer, {%HeroName%}.If we unite all the shards of the soul, the demon will be forced to manifest. Before we do that, we will need the rest of Evra's artifacts. Without them, we cannot face that demon again. We must travel to the Ferelden Coast for the shield. Fort Elim for the breastplate, and into the Deep Roads for the helm.
We must find and unite all shards of the demon's soul.
Retrieve Evra's Helm
Find Evra's artifact in the Deep Roads
Retrieve Evra's Breastplate
Find Evra's artifact at Fort Elim
Retrieve Evra's Shield
Find Evra's artifact on the Ferelden Coast
I have been thinking about that darkspawn we fought at {%castle_name%}...
You mean the one who TALKED?
I have heard of them before. They're Disciples, given sentience through blood magic.
He said he had learned what he needed. But what is it he learned? It's driving me crazy.
Lord Ravi! Kaiten is under attack!
Eiton! I left him there alone. {%HeroName%}, you must continue your search for the artifacts. This is imperative.
Tovez, Sendis, and I will go to Kaiten. You and Lukesh must find Evra's artifacts. Hurry and good luck.
We were too late. The enemy had already gone by the time we arrived.
Wait, they had gone? What of Kaiten?
Kaiten survives, but the prisoners escaped. Tianne, Soleil, Deymour, and the bone dragon.
I have a bad feeling about this.
It is good to be back at Fort Elim. All this time I defended it I had no idea it held such an important artifact.
I certainly didn't think we would ever need it again.
At least the bandits are gone. Where do we find Evra's Shield?
In the old watchtower. It should be in an upper room, under the floor boards.
If we see any spiders again, you go first.
Fine. So where did you put the helm?
Where did I... Oh right, that was me, wasn't it. Oh, it's got to be around here someplace.
I know those laughs... Tianne and Soliel?
Free as birds. We learned much while in the bowels of Kaiten.
Yes, we learned how much we hate you all. Especially you, {%HeroName%}.
It is a shared hatred. Now we shall share in your destruction.
This is it, this is Evra's Helm. We must find the other two artifacts now.
This is it, this is Evra's Breastplate. We must find the other two artifacts now.
This is it, this is Evra's Shield. We must find the other two artifacts now.
The Emissary said you would be here soon. I am so glad you did not disappoint.
Out of our way Deymour, or we will destroy you.
Destroy me? I have a friend I'd like to introduce. I believe you've met before?
Grraaaarrraaaak!
Excellent, now we have the Helm. Only one left. At least we've recaptured all the prisoners.
Excellent, now we have the Breastplate. Only one left. At least we've recaptured all the prisoners.
Excellent, now we have the Shield. Only one left. At least we've recaptured all the prisoners.
Who is this Emissary that Deymour spoke of?
The last shard of the demon's soul, perhaps? Why wouldn't he merge the five shards?
He is stronger like this. As long as the shards are separate, we cannot kill him.
Wait! I'm here to help.
What in blazes? Another talking darkspawn?
Yes. The first one you met... he is here, and he's set a trap...
Aieeee!
I see you have arrived for my party.
At least I have a consolation prize. The last piece of Evra's armor. You'll never...
Auugh!
It is done. The Disciple will plague neither you nor I anymore. Thank you for saving me. I must rest...
Leave The Prankster?
Leave The Prankster?
Leave the prankster here to die as a hero.
Your sacrifice will be remembered, Prankster.
Save The Prankster?
Save The Prankster?
Take him with you and try to save him.
Your wounds are grievous, but I might be able to save you.
You... would save me?
We've kept Antor around. You can't be all that much worse.
At least I have most of my skin left, though. Yuck.
Quest complete

The Prankster sacrificed himself to destroy the Darkspawn Disciple. Unfortunately, the Disciple was not the Emissary, whoever that is. You have found all of the pieces of Evra's armor and recaptured the four soul shards. Time to get them back to Kaiten.

The Prankster managed to kill the Darkspawn Disciple before he could escape. Unfortunately, the Disciple was not the Emissary, whoever that is. The Prankster is scarred by the Disciple's flames, but he only needs rest. You have found all of the pieces of Evra's armor and recaptured the four soul shards. Time to get them back to Kaiten.
This is a placeholder string. Please report where you encountered it.
Kaiten
While Lukesh attempts to divine the location of the last shard, you must prepare for the worst. Kaiten was badly damaged when it was last attacked. It needs to be repaired.
Return to Kaiten and hold off the invasion.
Repair Kaiten
Kaiten is badly damaged and is in great need of repair
Standoff
Confront the demon besieging Kaiten
A Tough Choice
Help Ravi make a decision
Kaiten has certainly seen better days.
It was fine until the demons came, I assure you.
Father. You have returned.
My son! Seeing your face raises my spirits.
That is good, father.
Lord Ravi, darkspawn have invaded the castle! Even more demons and darkspawn has been spotted approaching the city!
We must not waste any time. Quickly, we must fight through and patch the walls.
We'll have to use timbers to shore up this section. We don't have time for anything else.
Here is where the demons tore through the wall long ago.
Now that this is patched up, we have some hope of holding off such a scourge again.
Lord Ravi, the demons and darkspawn are upon us.
The city should hold so long as we are here. To the walls!
Eiton, get back inside. It is not safe out here for you.
Yes, father.
{%HeroName%}, to the north wall! Darkspawn are pouring over.
Looks like they brought some ogres.
Deal with them quickly. The gates cannot hold much longer against them.
More enemies are entering the walls south of here! Take them down, {%HeroName%}, and my men will hold off the ogres.
{%HeroName%}, I can help you. I am ready to fight.
Prankster! Your wounds are not yet healed, you must rest.
Let him help?
Let him help?
Allow The Prankster to help despite his wounds. (Cerlais and The Prankster will join you.)
Every little bit can help. Be careful, though.
Make him rest?
Make him rest?
Have Cerlais take him back and force him to rest. (Cerlais and The Prankster will NOT join you.)
You are not ready yet. There will be another day, Prankster.
Demons are coming from the southwest. They are pouring over into the city!
Magic users at the west wall. They are blasting it to bits! We'll be overrun.
{%HeroName%}, you take one side, I'll go with the other.
Help Tovez?
Help Tovez?
Fight off the demons at Tovez's side? (Sendis and Ravi will leave.)
Help Sendis?
Help Sendis?
Eliminate the magic users before they breach the wall? (Tovez and Ravi will leave.)
Be careful, and good luck.
Lord Ravi is in trouble! He is getting overwhelmed!
You help Ravi. I can hold the rest of these genlocks off.
You help Ravi. I have these darkspawn at bay.
Tovez! Oh, this is tragic!
Sendis! Oh, this is tragic!
I can heal him. Just keep these darkspawn off of me.
I will get him to Cerlais. The rest of you fight on.
Over there, that demon in the distance is leading this horde. We need to take him down.
What a lovely reunion this is.
I can't say we've met before.
Do you not recognize your uncle Khedra?
All that was left of my uncle was the demon. YOU. And now you will pay for his death.
What? You cannot defeat me. I shall haunt you until you are destroyed!
Lukesh! The others, bring them now!
Wait, nooooOOOOOarrraaaaagggggh!
We must defeat the demon while it is weakened from rejoining the souls.
The killing blow... wait, Eiton? What are you doing out here?
Father, I must go. I must be... released.
The last piece of the soul... it is Eiton. Iselle was pregnant with him when the demon was shattered?
Destroy me and save Kaiten...
...If you can! I shall consume you all!
Release us into the Fade, father. I will bind it there.
Grrarraarrrrrr!!!
Have Ravi kill it?
Have Ravi kill it?
Have Ravi kill the demon and his son Eiton in the process?
Kill my own son? And all I have left of Iselle? I can't do it.
Eiton is not Iselle. In a way, she sacrificed herself and Eiton to defeat the demon long ago.
If you refuse to finish it now, it will only bring more tragedy.
You are right. Goodbye Eiton, goodbye Iselle.
Kill the demon yourself?
Kill the demon yourself?
Kill the demon yourself, killing Eiton in the process?
I cannot do it. My only son, and all I have left of Iselle...
I am sorry, my friend. This is how it must be.
I cannot do it. You must {%HeroName%}! Goodbye Eiton. Goodbye Iselle, my love.
Goodbye father...
Story complete

My son is dead, but he is free from the demon that tormented him. I cannot thank you enough for what you have done, {%HeroName%}, despite the great costs. Kaiten - and Thedas - is a safer place because of your deeds.
